title: bupleurum ranunculoides subvar. ramosum
intro: Bupleurum ranunculoides subvar. ramosum, also known as Chinese thickstem or thickstem bupleurum, is a type of flowering plant in the carrot family, native to China and used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. It is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving joint pain, and improving immune function. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be taken orally or used topically for various health issues. Traditional Chinese medicine has employed Bupleurum ranunculoides subvar. ramosum for centuries to treat a range of conditions, including digestive problems, respiratory issues, and skin conditions.
